summary: The review of the *Student Research Agreement Example 2.pdf* indicates a moderate risk posture, with several clauses requiring attention. Key issues include misalignment with UoA policies on Intellectual Property (POS-IP), confidentiality (POS-11), and publication rights (POS-12), as well as incomplete or ambiguous drafting in critical areas. Notably, clauses addressing IP ownership, thesis embargoes, and publication restrictions deviate from UoA's preferred positions, necessitating escalation to Auckland UniServices Ltd and the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for resolution. The next step is to prioritize redrafting flagged clauses, ensuring compliance with UoA policies and clarity in contractual obligations.

flags.clause_title: 6. The Research is to be primarily undertaken at the
flags.snippet: 6. The Research is to be primarily undertaken at the “Confidential Information” means any information relating Location. to the business or financial affairs of the University, Obligations of the Student the existence a…
flags.rationale: The clause text appears to be incomplete and conflates two unrelated topics: the location of research and the definition of Confidential Information. This creates ambiguity and does not align with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions on either topic. For Confidentiality, the clause does not clearly exclude public, independently developed, or previously known information, nor does it specify a duration for confidentiality obligations. For the location of research, it does not specify whether the research is to be conducted in New Zealand or an acceptable alternative jurisdiction. Both issues require escalation for clarification and redrafting.
flags.standard_ref: UoA Position #POS-11 (Confidentiality) and #POS-01 (Insurance — Location of Project Delivery)

flags.clause_title: 14. The Stipend (if any) and the Fees (if any) shall be paid as
flags.snippet: 14. The Stipend (if any) and the Fees (if any) shall be paid as embargo of the Thesis will be reviewed every two years set out in the Schedule. and will cease where it is no longer reasonable to require continued embargo.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses the embargo of a thesis, which directly impacts the UoA-sensitive topic of Publication. UoA's Preferred Position (POS-12) allows students to retain publication rights for their thesis or academic publication, including commercially sensitive content, with a reasonable patent stand-down of no more than 60 days. The Acceptable Position permits embargoes for commercially sensitive content for a maximum of 12 months. However, this clause introduces a two-year review cycle for the embargo, which implies a potential embargo duration exceeding 12 months. This materially deviates from the Acceptable Position and requires escalation to the School of Graduate Studies for review and approval.

flags.clause_title: 16. The Student and the University acknowledge and agree
flags.snippet: 16. The Student and the University acknowledge and agree the Student: that it is a condition of the Student’s involvement in the 29.1. Grants to a royalty-free, non-exclusive right to Project that the Results, including…
flags.rationale: This clause appears to assign exclusive ownership of all Intellectual Property (IP) created by the Student to the Client, which conflicts with UoA's IP policy. UoA Positions explicitly state that IP matters must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (POS-IP). Additionally, the clause touches on publication rights but does not clearly preserve the Student's right to publish their thesis or academic work, which is a critical requirement under UoA Position #POS-12. This clause requires escalation to Auckland UniServices Ltd for IP review and to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for publication rights review.

flags.clause_title: 17. Subject to clause 29, the Student and the University agree
flags.snippet: 17. Subject to clause 29, the Student and the University agree Publication on the Research is produced by the Student. to sign all documents and do all things reasonably 31. will not withhold permission for the Student or the University to publish any Publication provided that: 31.1. The Publication does not in any way interfere, hinder or damage commercial interests, or prevent from protecting any Intellectual Property rights.
flags.rationale: The clause materially deviates from UoA Position #POS-12 on Publication. While it acknowledges the Student's right to publish, it imposes conditions that could hinder academic freedom, such as requiring publications to not interfere with commercial interests or IP protection. This conflicts with the Preferred Position, which ensures students retain publication rights for theses or academic publications, including commercially sensitive content, and limits embargoes to a maximum of 12 months. The clause's ambiguity and potential for extended restrictions necessitate escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review.

flags.clause_title: 18. If exploitation of the Results by requires access to the
flags.snippet: 18. If exploitation of the Results by requires access to the arising from the Project; University’s Background Intellectual Property, then 31.2. The Student or the University, as the case may be, and the University agree to negotiate in good faith with the provides with a copy of the proposed aim of reaching agreement on a licence to use the Publication at least twenty (20) Working Days prior to the date of submission for publication; and 31.3. The Student or the University, as the case may be, Confidentiality makes all reasonable changes, amendments,
flags.rationale: The clause text is incoherent and appears to conflate multiple topics, including Background Intellectual Property licensing, publication review timelines, and confidentiality obligations. This creates ambiguity and potential misalignment with UoA's Preferred Positions. Specifically, Background IP licensing must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (POS-IP), and publication review timelines must align with POS-12 (30 days for review, 60 days for patent stand-down). The clause does not clearly adhere to these standards and requires escalation to both UniServices (for IP) and the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts (for publication).

flags.clause_title: 19. The Parties will keep confidential, and will not use, or
flags.snippet: 19. The Parties will keep confidential, and will not use, or alternations or deletions to the Publication as permit to be used, any Confidential Information that is requested by prior to submitting the Publication. disc…
flags.rationale: The clause appears to address confidentiality but is poorly drafted, making its intent unclear. It does not align with UoA's Preferred Position #POS-11, which requires a clear definition of confidential information, exclusions for public or independently developed information, and a time limit (up to 5 years post-completion). The clause also seems to conflate confidentiality with publication, which is a separate topic under UoA Position #POS-12. This ambiguity and lack of alignment with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable tiers necessitate escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for clarification and potential redrafting.

flags.clause_title: 22. Discussions between the Student and the University
flags.snippet: 22. Discussions between the Student and the University is unable to provide another suitable research project Supervisor regarding the Research are expressly for the Student, then may terminate this Agreement authorised…
flags.rationale: The clause text is incomplete and incoherent, making it impossible to determine whether it aligns with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions on Confidentiality (POS-11). The clause also appears to mix topics (termination and confidentiality) in a way that is not standard or clear. This requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for clarification and redrafting.

flags.clause_title: 23.3. The Student makes all reasonable changes to the
flags.snippet: 23.3. The Student makes all reasonable changes to the to do so; or report as requested by 34.1.3. Commits an act of serious misconduct which, in reasonable opinion, brings, Thesis or may bring, the Research, the Project
flags.rationale: The clause text is incomplete, incoherent, and appears to conflate multiple topics, including publication rights and misconduct. This creates ambiguity and potential misalignment with UoA Position #POS-12 on Publication, which requires clear preservation of the student's right to publish their thesis or academic work. The clause also lacks clarity on the scope of 'reasonable changes' and the process for addressing misconduct, which could lead to disputes. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required to ensure compliance with UoA's publication policies and to address the clause's deficiencies.

flags.clause_title: 24. The Student shall provide with a copy of the Thesis in
flags.snippet: 24. The Student shall provide with a copy of the Thesis in into disrepute. the form the Student intends to submit for examination at 34.2. The Student: least twenty (20) Working Days prior to the due date for 34.2.1. Fa…
flags.rationale: The clause conflicts with UoA Position #POS-16 on Governing Law and Jurisdiction. The preferred governing law is New Zealand, with acceptable alternatives being Australia, the United Kingdom, Common European countries, or Singapore. For US-based clients, non-exclusive jurisdiction of a specific US state is acceptable. This clause does not specify the governing law or jurisdiction, creating ambiguity and potential misalignment with UoA's preferred and acceptable positions. Escalation to General Counsel is required.

flags.clause_title: 2. The Student agrees to undertake the Research in relation
flags.snippet: 2. The Student agrees to undertake the Research in relation “Background Intellectual Property” means any Intellectual to the Project commencing on the Commencement Date Property belonging to a Party or which a Party may...
flags.rationale: The clause addresses Intellectual Property (IP) by defining 'Background Intellectual Property.' UoA Contracting Positions explicitly state that all IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices (Head of IP) for review, as UoA does not set IP positions independently. This clause falls under the Acceptable tier for IP clauses, requiring referral.

flags.clause_title: 7.3. Undertake the Research using proper and
flags.snippet: 7.3. Undertake the Research using proper and Intellectual Property means any property right recognised scientific and research standards; constituted under the Patents Act 2013, the Designs Act
flags.rationale: The clause references 'Intellectual Property,' which falls under UoA Position #POS-IP. UoA Contracting Positions explicitly state that all IP-related clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review, regardless of wording. This clause is flagged as AMBER because it requires referral to UniServices for compliance verification.

flags.clause_title: 15. and the University shall continue to own all rights in
flags.snippet: 15. and the University shall continue to own all rights in University examination process. their respective Background Intellectual Property. 29. The Student shall retain copyright in the Thesis, provided
flags.rationale: The clause pertains to Intellectual Property, which is explicitly excluded from UoA Contracting Positions and must be referred to Auckland UniServices (Head of IP) for review. This aligns with UoA Position #POS-IP, which mandates referral of all IP clauses regardless of wording.

flags.clause_title: 20. The Parties shall continue to keep the Confidential
flags.snippet: 20. The Parties shall continue to keep the Confidential Student by name where the Student has contributed Information confidential following the expiry or termination materially to the research being published. of this Agreement, until such time as the Confidential Information lawfully enters the public domain.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses confidentiality obligations but does not specify a time limit for the confidentiality period, which is a key requirement under UoA Position #POS-11. The Preferred Position requires a defined duration (up to 5 years post-completion), and the Acceptable Position allows for 5–7 years. The absence of a time limit makes this clause fall outside the Preferred and Acceptable tiers, requiring escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review.

flags.rationale: The clause addresses Intellectual Property, which is outside the scope of UoA Contracting Positions. All IP clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices (Head of IP) for review, as per UoA Position #POS-IP.
